CONTENT.Early life: Guilavogui was born in Ollioules, France, to a Guinean father and Moroccan mother.
CONTENT.Club career: On 17 May 2020, Guilavogui signed a professional contract with Paris FC. He made his professional debut with the club in a 2–1 Ligue 2 win over Amiens on 12 September 2020.On 26 July 2024, Guilavogui moved on loan to FC St. Pauli in Germany.
CONTENT.Personal life: Guilavogui is the brother of the French international footballer Josuha Guilavogui.
CONTENT.External links: Morgan Guilavogui at SoccerwayParis FC ProfileAnciens Verts Profile
CONTENT.Career statistics: As of match played 19 May 2024As of match played 21 November 2023
CONTENT.International career: Guilavogui holds French and Guinean nationalities. He debuted with the Guinea national team in a 0–0 2022 FIFA World Cup qualification tie against Guinea Bissau on 12 November 2021.

SUMMARY: Morgan Bono Guilavogui (born 10 March 1998) is a professional footballer who plays as a midfielder for German Bundesliga club FC St. Pauli on loan from Lens. Born in France, he plays for the Guinea national team.
